topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and unwelcome, on my phone screen. A picture of me, clearly taken from a recent school event, was tagged in a post by Mr. Harrison. My jaw tightened. I stared at the screen, a slow burn starting low in my chest. He hadn't just liked the photo; he’d added a caption about my 'excellent presentation skills.' I felt a hot flush creep up my neck. I immediately locked the screen, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to steady my breathing. This felt entirely inappropriate. I walked over to my desk, gripping my pen so hard my knuckles were white. I needed to confront this absurdity.

[story 2]

I scrolled through my feed, and there it was: Mr. Davies’ profile picture, next to a recent photo of me from the history club meeting. A little caption beneath it suggested he was 'always looking out for his brightest students.' A sharp, unpleasant feeling settled in my stomach, making my hands clench into fists under the desk. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing in the quiet classroom. I couldn't just ignore it. I stood up abruptly, my chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor. I felt a surge of heat rise, making my face feel tight and hot. I needed to know why he was doing this.

[story 3]

It was late, and I was browsing Instagram, mindlessly scrolling through old posts, when I saw the tag. Mr. Evans. He’d posted a story—a quick video clip from the previous week—and I was clearly in it. A sudden, jarring sensation of irritation washed over me, making my shoulders tense up near my ears. I physically recoiled from the phone, my breath catching in my throat. I looked around the empty library, feeling suddenly exposed. I tapped the screen repeatedly, my thumb pressing against the glass as if trying to break the connection. A deep furrow formed between my eyebrows; this was unacceptable behavior.
